sections.content:
Now I see the indicator area clearly:
Row 42: BBBWWwwwWWBBBWWwwwWWwwwWW
Row 44: WBWWWWwWWWWBWWWWwWWWWwWWW
Row 45: WBWWWWwWWWWBWWWWwWWWWwWWW
Row 46: WBWWWWwWWWWBWWWWwWWWWwWWW
There are 5 columns of indicators:
- Col 20-22: B (black) horizontal bar at row 42, B vertical bar at rows 44-46
- Col 25-27: w (light gray) horizontal bar at row 42, w vertical bar at rows 44-46
- Col 30-32: B (black) horizontal bar at row 42, B vertical bar at rows 44-46
- Col 35-37: w (light gray) horizontal bar at row 42, w vertical bar at rows 44-46
- Col 40-42: w (light gray) horizontal bar at row 42, w vertical bar at rows 44-46
These look like buttons! Each button has a horizontal bar (row 42) and a vertical stem (rows 44-46). The pattern is:
- Button 1 (col 20-22): B (black) - horizontal + vertical
- Button 2 (col 25-27): w (gray) - horizontal + vertical
- Button 3 (col 30-32): B (black) - horizontal + vertical
- Button 4 (col 35-37): w (gray) - horizontal + vertical
- Button 5 (col 40-42): w (gray) - horizontal + vertical
Looking at the image, these look like piano keys or musical notes! The black keys (B) are shorter and the white keys (w) are taller.
Wait, actually looking more carefully:
- Row 42 has the horizontal bars: B at cols 20-22, w at cols 25-27, B at cols 30-32, w at cols 35-37, w at cols 40-42
- Rows 44-46 have vertical stems: B at col 21, w at col 26, B at col 31, w at col 36, w at col 41
This looks like a piano keyboard or musical notation interface. The B-colored ones might represent black keys and w-colored ones might represent white keys.
Given the shapes on the board:
- Top shape: A solid block with a gap (like a filled rectangle with a hole)
- Bottom shape: A U-shape
Maybe the goal is to use these buttons to transform the shapes? Or maybe the buttons correspond to different operations?
Let me try clicking on one of the buttons to see what happens. I'll start with the first black button at (42, 21).

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Pattern: B, w, B, w, w (black, gray, black, gray, gray)
# These look like piano keys or interactive buttons
# Let me try clicking on the first black button at (42, 21)

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 42, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
